payload.answer: Recall returned mem_new because it is the active, current memory record for Sam from Goldman. mem_old was filtered because it is explicitly marked as superseded—a durable memory system must not present obsolete facts as current, ensuring recall accuracy and preventing contradictory information.
citations: Memory supersession protocol: newer records replace older ones
Recall filtering rule: exclude superseded memories from results
Durable memory principle: never present deleted/superseded facts as current
payload.decision: mem_new is current; mem_old is superseded and filtered from recall results

entity_resolution.reason: mem_new represents the authoritative current state; mem_old is obsolete and filtered to prevent stale information in recall results

payload.intent: Explain recall filtering logic for Sam from Goldman: why mem_new was returned and mem_old was filtered as superseded

memory_cards.statement: mem_new contains current information about Sam from Goldman
mem_old is marked as superseded by mem_new

details: No ambiguity preserved in this case—clear supersession chain

repair_options: Verify mem_new contains complete and accurate information about Sam
Confirm supersession link between mem_old and mem_new is correctly recorded
Archive mem_old with supersession metadata for audit trail
